chunks.text: HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No. D-1617 of 2025 [Abdul Aziz vs. Province of Sindh and Others] BEFORE: JUSTICE ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON JUSTICE RIAZAT ALI SAHAR Petitioner: Through Mr. Waseem Abbas advocate Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri Assistant A.G Date of hearing & Decision: 16.12.2025 O R D E R ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON, J. - Through present petition, the petitioner seeks directions against the respondent police authorities to issue his appointment order and allow him to join duty for the post of Police Constable (BPS-07). 2. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that, pursuant to advertisement dated 24.03.2024, the petitioner applied for the post of Police Constable, successfully qualified for all requisite tests and interview, and was consequently issued an offer letter dated 27.06.2025. However, he was subsequently declared medically unfit on the allegation of Hepatitis ‘C’. Counsel further submits that the petitioner thereafter underwent medical examination at Agha Khan Laboratory, and the report dated 19.08.2025 issued by the said laboratory confirms that Hepatitis-C virus RNA was not detected in the Petitioner blood. 3. This court vide order dated 20.11.2025, issued directions to the police authorities to obtain fresh medical report of the petitioner from Agha Khan Laboratory by deputing their representative. However, in terms of the order dated 15.12.2025, no such report has been submitted. However petitioner, in compliance with the aforesaid order, has submitted fresh report on the subject issue and seeks disposal of the petition in terms thereof. 4. We have heard learned counsel for the Petitioner as well as learned Additional Advocate General of Sindh, and have examined the material placed on record, including the medical report dated 19.08.2025 issued by Agha Khan Laboratory, which reflects that Hepatitis-C virus was not detected in the Petitioner blood.

However petitioner, in compliance with the aforesaid order, has submitted fresh report on the subject issue and seeks disposal of the petition in terms thereof. 4. We have heard learned counsel for the Petitioner as well as learned Additional Advocate General of Sindh, and have examined the material placed on record, including the medical report dated 19.08.2025 issued by Agha Khan Laboratory, which reflects that Hepatitis-C virus was not detected in the Petitioner blood. 5. Admittedly, the Petitioner had successfully cleared all stages of the recruitment process. His candidature was thereafter withdrawn solely on the ground that, during medical screening, he was declared suffering from Hepatitis C reactive. Now fresh medical report issued by Agha Khan Laboratory reflects that Hepatitis-C virus was not detected in the Petitioner blood. Since the main impediment has been cured and the petitioner is medically fit, as such, this Court is left with no option but to allow the petition and cannot wait for the decision of scrutiny committee to decide the fate of the petitioner, which has already been delayed for considerable period of time. 6. Consequently, this petition is allowed, and the Inspector General of Police, Sindh is directed to issue the appointment letter in favour of the petitioner for the subject post forthwith, subject to fulfilling the codal formalities as required under the law. In case of non-compliance, coercive action shall be taken against the delinquent officers. A copy of this order shall be communicated to IGP Sindh for compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Karar_Hussain/PS*
